scope: Long-document chunking risk review based on the saved NIST AI RMF online API run.

risks.current_observation: All saved chunks completed but reported document-level provenance and `no_page_provenance`.
risks.impact: Reviewers cannot audit field-level answers to exact pages or bboxes on this online API path.
risks.mitigation: Use local MinerU CLI for audit-grade PDF runs, or rerun with an API variant that emits page/block provenance.

risks.current_observation: The online API page limit forces 1-20, 21-40, and 41-48 page ranges.
risks.impact: References that start in one chunk and resolve in another need a merge/review layer.
risks.mitigation: Keep chunk manifests, expose page ranges in retrieval metadata, and add cross-chunk entity/table merge tests.

risks.current_observation: The saved long-document run covers one 48-page public PDF.
risks.impact: It validates the orchestration path, not long-document accuracy across many formats.
risks.mitigation: Add 100+ page annual reports and standards with field/table labels in the benchmark set.

risks.current_observation: This artifact is an online API chunking run, not local MinerU CLI/GPU throughput.
risks.impact: GPU pages/second and local artifact cost remain environment-specific.
risks.mitigation: Run the same script in the HeyWhale MinerU GPU image and record pages/second, GPU hours, and page provenance coverage.

reviewer_takeaway: The saved run shows the Agent can split, execute, and aggregate a 48-page public PDF across a 20-page API limit.
It should be read as orchestration evidence, not as a full long-document accuracy benchmark.
